[[Category:grammatical numbers]]
The '''plentiful''' is a grammatical number in [[Aelaki]] used to express a large quantity or abundance of something.

== Formation ==

The plentiful is related to the [[Paucal|paucal]] in that both express quantity along a scale, but while the paucal indicates "a few," the plentiful indicates "many" or "a large number."

== Relationship to Other Numbers ==

The plentiful occupies the high end of the quantity scale among the six grammatical numbers in Aelaki.

{| class="wikitable"
! Number !! Quantity !! Morphological Strategy
|-
| [[Singular]] || one || Base template (unmarked)
|-
| [[Paucal]] || a few || Inanimate plural marker (ïfïf)
|-
| [[Aelaki plural|Plural]] || some of || Reduplication of C₂
|-
| '''Plentiful''' || many || Abundance marker
|-
| [[Collective]] || all || Umlaut (vowel fronting)
|-
| [[Zero]] || none || Zero-infix /f/
|}

The plentiful and [[Paucal|paucal]] together form a pair of quantity-oriented numbers that complement the more structurally defined [[Aelaki plural|plural]] (partitive) and [[Collective|collective]] (totality).

== See Also ==

* [[Paucal]]
* [[Aelaki plural|Plural]]
* [[Singular]]
* [[Collective]]
* [[Zero]]
